steps.title: Pin down the time frame
steps.content: The passage establishes the Dust Bowl as "a period of severe drought" during the 1930s — a finished historical event. The following sentence also uses simple past ("They even reached as far east as New York City").

steps.title: Match verb tense to a completed past event
steps.content: An action completed in the 1930s calls for the simple past tense. "Affected" is the simple past form of "affect."

steps.title: Eliminate the other choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — are affecting: present progressive describes ongoing action right now, contradicting the 1930s time frame. ✗
  • Choice B — will have affected: future perfect describes an action completed before some future point, which makes no sense for an event that already happened. ✗
  • Choice C — will affect: simple future projects forward, but the dust storms are a past event, not a future one. ✗
  • Choice D — affected: simple past correctly matches the 1930s time frame and parallels "reached" in the next sentence. ✓
That matches Choice D — affected.
